intended_use: The CLARUS 700 ophthalmic camera is indicated to capture, display, annotate and store images to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of diseases and disorders occurring in the retina, ocular surface an
intended_use_full: The CLARUS 700 ophthalmic camera is indicated to capture, display, annotate and store images to aid in the diagnosis and monitoring of diseases and disorders occurring in the retina, ocular surface and visible adnexa. It provides true color and autofluorescence imaging modes for stereo, widefield, ultra-widefield, and montage fields of view. The CLARUS 700 angiography is indicated as an aid in the visualization of vascular structures of the retina and the choroid.
target_condition: Diseases and disorders of the retina, ocular surface, and visible adnexa; vitreoretinal and choroidal diseases; diabetic retinopathy; retinal vein and artery occlusions
ground_truth: Clinical assessment of image quality and utility for fluorescein angiography in detecting areas and lesions of interest in vitreoretinal and choroidal diseases
other_metrics: Fluorescein Angiography image quality passing rates: Area and lesion of interest visible 17/20 (85%); Clinically useful image 19/20 (95%); Artifacts do not interfere with interpretation 19/20 (95%)
